title: sideritis montana subsp. ebracteata
intro: Sideritis montana subsp. ebracteata, also known as the Ebracteate mountain tea or mountain tea, is a subspecies of the Sideritis montana plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the mint family native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Sideritis montana subsp. ebracteata include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and boosting the immune system. It can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
